text:
Market today: Tech is bouncing — cybersecurity names leading, Alphabet up 4% on its Dow debut. The real driver is easing fear around Fed independence (Cook stays for now) and a soft-dollar narrative helping growth stocks. Your chip/tech concentration is in a decent spot today, but margin means you can't afford to be complacent on the losers.

Your holdings: AVGO (-15.1%) is your largest position and still flagged TRIM — a 15% loss on a big, margined slot is real pain, watch the $336 stop hard. PLTR (-11%) is flagged SELL — down double digits with no clear catalyst to reverse, margin amplifies every down day. PTNM (-15.3%) is a tiny spec already deep in the hole near its $9.50 stop — easiest cut if you need to reduce exposure. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited data; treat it as fragile.

Watch: Friday's jobs report — Kalshi traders expect a miss, which could shake rate expectations and slam growth/tech. Monitor AVGO price action around $336.

Bottom line: Cut or right-size PLTR and AVGO before a weak jobs number forces your hand on margin.
